comparable_to_six_routing_test.note: the #6 named-only-band routing test deflated: m1/m2/m3 routing on <=2/1/2 of 5 plural grades

summary: Routable-population test, Burst D (2015Q3->2015Q4), plural subprime grades P=['C1', 'C2', 'C5', 'D4'] (n=4; flagged=['A1', 'A5', 'B1', 'C1', 'C2', 'C5', 'D4'], all-plural=['A1', 'A5', 'B1', 'C1', 'C2', 'C5', 'D4']): P5 (quality split survives used-feature-set de-dup) HIT (subprime R2_named<0.3 on 3/4; prime well-explained: {'A1': True, 'A5': True, 'B1': True}); P1a (r-terciles route: m3 AND m4 on >maj) MISS (m3 yes/no=[2, 2], m4 yes/no=[1, 3]); P1b (consensus worse on policy-blind half on >maj) MISS (2/4); P1 headline = MISS; P2 (explained part deflates) MISS (m3 on d_hat yes/no=[3, 1]); P4 (residual >= raw) HIT (123-yes r=1 vs d_ext=0). VERDICT: NO ROUTABLE POPULATION -- per-case disagreement-routing is dead even with the wider feature view and the policy-blind decomposition; the extension-admitted finding is about observability (the policy's vocabulary is inadequate to the indeterminacy here), not triage [CAVEAT: the explained-part control did NOT deflate -- the residual decomposition may be confounded; see P2].
